name: Master-Detail
when_to_use: Use when users browse a list of homogeneous items and need to inspect or edit one at a time while keeping the list in view for context and rapid switching, such as inboxes, file browsers, settings, or record managers.
when_not_to_use: Avoid when the detail content is wider than the viewport can spare alongside the list, when items are highly heterogeneous, or when users almost always act on the list as a whole rather than drilling into single items.
information_structure: A two-pane parent-child relationship: a persistent list (master) of peer items on one side and a contextual detail view of the selected item on the other. Selection in the master drives the content of the detail.
navigation: Selecting a list item updates the detail pane in place without a full page change; keyboard up/down should move selection and the detail should follow.
responsive_behaviour: On narrow viewports collapse to a single column: show the list first, then push or replace it with the detail on selection, providing a clear back affordance to return to the list.
accessibility_notes: Manage focus when the detail updates so screen-reader users are informed the content changed
Expose the master as a list with proper roles and announce the selected state
Ensure the collapsed mobile back navigation is keyboard-operable and preserves scroll position
evidence: Apple HIG and Material Design both document master-detail (list-detail) as a canonical pattern for browsing collections
Email clients (Gmail, Outlook) and IDEs validate the pattern at scale
